sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports left-sided back pain, primarily with a history of recurrent strep infections and frequent sore throats. He also reports experiencing an increase in urine output and notes that his brother and other family members have been experiencing similar illnesses.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals no cervical lymphadenopathy, no wheezes or rales, and normal heart sounds. Laboratory results will include a lipid panel, UA, CMP, CBC, Vitamin D, and TSH to assess hormone levels and iron status.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The physical exam findings, combined with the patient's history of recurrent strep infections, suggest a possible diagnosis of glomerulonephritis and secondary kidney disease. The patient's symptoms warrant further investigation and management.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The physician ordered a CT scan to evaluate the findings, referred the patient to an ENT specialist for consideration of tonsillectomy, and ordered a comprehensive laboratory panel including lipid panel, UA, CMP, CBC, Vitamin D, and TSH. The physician will discuss the importance of dietary sodium reduction given the potential for kidney complications and scheduled a follow-up appointment in two weeks.
